subject: Returning null for unresolved variables
commit_message:
Returning null for unresolved variables

When Blueprints Processor was not able to evaluate a variable, it would
set its value to null.

The expected behaviour would be to set the value to the default repres-
entation in the formal notation as defined by Apache Velocity, which is
a dollar followed by the name of the variable between curly braces. For
example, if the value of the variable pnf-id could not be evaluated in
runtime, its value would be defined as the string "${pnf-id}".

The problem happened during evaluation of the variables that would be
later sent to the template-meshing code for processing.

The fix was to add a check before the value was assigned to the varia-
ble; if the was not null, the assignment will happen normally. Otherwi-
se, if the evaluation resolves to null, the variable receives the defa-
ult value (string "${<variable name>}").

Besides the tests that were put in place to test the code changed for
this fix, two tests were added to the existing test case of the templa-
te meshing code, to act as regression test.

Change-Id: I635afb1eba4c0d45b821811f0119fa6c87ea9542
Issue-ID: CCSDK-1358
Signed-off-by: ottero <rodrigo.ottero@est.tech>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Verify build is failing

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Verify build is failing

The build is breaking because of the extra tests added to BluePrintTemplateServiceTest.

The tests create an instance of the class BluePrintTemplateService, whose constructor changed in 17/05, commit bcc6c4. The new constructor is parameterless:

 class BluePrintTemplateService : BlueprintTemplateService {

whereas the version in Dublin has a previous version, which has a constructor with parameters:

 class BluePrintTemplateService(val bluePrintRuntimeService: BluePrintRuntimeService<*>,
                                val nodeTemplateName: String, val artifactName: String):
        BlueprintTemplateService {

Because of that, the extra tests are not compiling:

 18:46:29 [ERROR] Failed to execute goal org.jetbrains.kotlin:kotlin-maven-plugin:1.3.21:test-compile (test-compile) on project blueprint-core: Compilation failure: Compilation failure:
 18:46:29 [ERROR] /w/workspace/ccsdk-cds-dublin-verify-java/ms/controllerblueprints/modules/blueprint-core/src/test/kotlin/org/onap/ccsdk/cds/controllerblueprints/core/service/BluePrintTemplateServiceTest.kt:[120,69] No value passed for  parameter 'bluePrintRuntimeService'
 18:46:29 [ERROR] /w/workspace/ccsdk-cds-dublin-verify-java/ms/controllerblueprints/modules/blueprint-core/src/test/kotlin/org/onap/ccsdk/cds/controllerblueprints/core/service/BluePrintTemplateServiceTest.kt:[120,69] No value passed for parameter 'nodeTemplateName'
 18:46:29 [ERROR] /w/workspace/ccsdk-cds-dublin-verify-java/ms/controllerblueprints/modules/blueprint-core/src/test/kotlin/org/onap/ccsdk/cds/controllerblueprints/core/service/BluePrintTemplateServiceTest.kt:[120,69] No value passed for parameter 'artifactName'

These extra tests were added for regression purposes and are not directly related with the changes done in this patch, so removing them won't affect the patch quality.

My suggestion would be to just merge the first two files:

 ms/blueprintsprocessor/functions/resource-resolution/src/main/kotlin/org/onap/ccsdk/cds/blueprintsprocessor/functions/resource/resolution/utils/ResourceAssignmentUtils.kt 
 ms/blueprintsprocessor/functions/resource-resolution/src/test/kotlin/org/onap/ccsdk/cds/blueprintsprocessor/functions/resource/resolution/utils/ResourceAssignmentUtilsTest.kt

As I said, the purpose of the other files is to add more regression coverage to the tests.
